what_it_does: A command-line consumer that reads JSONL session/transcript files, passes each event through a declaration layer (runtime.render_declaration), and renders the resulting declaration records as plain text. It can also scan fixed corpus glob patterns of real transcripts and test captures, tallying render_key coverage and failing if any event is undeclared.
observations: Imports runtime.render_declaration as rd and calls rd.declare(ev) for every JSON line.
render_one builds one textual line per declaration from component, placement, fields, optional flags, and recursively renders nested blocks indented.
Skips field keys ending in __truncated, escapes newlines, JSON-encodes non-string values, and truncates strings over 120 characters.
Flags rendered lines with <undeclared>, <family_fallback>, <blocks_execution>, or <is_terminal> when present in the declaration.
render_file tallies events, rendered lines, non-JSON lines, undeclared keys, family-fallback keys, and per-render_key counts.
scan_corpus globs ~/.claude/projects/*/*.jsonl and ~/xsession-tests/**/*.jsonl, renders each quietly, aggregates counts, and exits 1 if any undeclared event appears.
main dispatches --scan-corpus or renders each supplied file, printing per-file summaries and returning 1 for undeclared events.
standouts: Proof-oriented design: the file explicitly avoids inspecting raw event types and treats successful rendering as evidence that the declaration registry is complete.
scan_corpus is a completeness gate over real-world data, not just a demo renderer.
Family fallback is surfaced as registry gap-pressure rather than silently accepted.
conventions: Docstring includes usage, exit codes, and R1.2 proof framing.
Uses os.path.dirname chain plus sys.path.insert to reach the parent package.
Uses glob with os.path.expanduser and recursive=True for corpus discovery.
Uses json.dumps for non-string field values and f-strings for output formatting.
concerns: scan_corpus catches all Exceptions and continues, so file-level failures do not fail the proof run.
render_one recursively calls itself on blocks without validating block shape.
Rendered output and summary diagnostics are both written to stdout, which could complicate piping.
No explicit validation that rd.declare returns a dict or contains expected keys.
notes: A CLI proof harness that renders session streams purely from declarations and scans real corpora to enforce zero undeclared events.
questions: What exact contract does runtime.render_declaration.declare provide?
What do the flags family_fallback, blocks_execution, and is_terminal signify semantically?
What is a T2 stream capture and how does its schema differ from a transcript?
What is the intended structure of declaration blocks and how deeply can they nest?
What is a render_key and how is it assigned by the declaration layer?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual behavior: it renders from declarations only and scans real streams to verify complete coverage.
apparent_themes: separation of event semantics from presentation
test harness as a correctness gate
intention_signals: The file is trying to demonstrate that the declaration layer alone is sufficient to consume and display every real event, using a corpus scan to pressure the registry into complete coverage.
novelty: It turns a renderer into a verification tool: rendering every real event through declarations and treating any undeclared event as a hard failure, while flagging family fallback as a coverage gap.
contribution: Provides a deterministic, declaration-only consumer and corpus scanner that renders events to text and reports whether the declaration registry fully covers observed real-world streams.
summary_for_embedding: A Python CLI that renders JSONL session streams from declaration records and scans real transcript corpora to verify every event is declared.
intention_for_embedding: To prove and enforce that the declaration registry can completely render all real event streams without inspecting raw event types.

symbols.does: Renders a single declaration record into a plain-text line, including flags and recursively rendered blocks.
symbols.description: Takes a declaration dict, reads component, placement, fields, and optional status flags, formats field values with truncation and newline escaping, and returns a textual representation plus indented lines for any nested blocks.

symbols.does: Reads a JSONL file, declares and renders each event, and returns a coverage tally.
symbols.description: Opens the file, parses each non-empty line as JSON, calls the declaration layer, renders the result, prints rendered text unless quiet, and accumulates counts of events, render_keys, undeclared events, and family-fallback events.

symbols.does: Scans predefined real transcript and test capture globs and reports aggregate coverage, failing on undeclared events.
symbols.description: Collects JSONL files from ~/.claude/projects and ~/xsession-tests, renders each quietly, aggregates per-render_key counts and undeclared/family-fallback hits, prints a summary, and returns 1 if any undeclared event is found.

symbols.does: CLI entry point that dispatches --scan-corpus or renders each supplied file.
symbols.description: Parses argv, runs corpus scan if requested, otherwise renders each path, prints per-file summaries, and exits with 1 if any file contains undeclared events.
